docs.title: Business Context
docs.text: RetailCo wants to modernize their product catalog system to support their growing e-commerce business. They need a centralized system to manage product information, track inventory across multiple warehouses, and provide customers with a seamless browsing experience.

docs.title: Business Challenge
docs.text:
Currently, RetailCo faces several challenges:

  • Product information is scattered across multiple spreadsheets
  • Inventory levels are updated manually, leading to overselling
  • Customers can't see real-time product availability
  • Search functionality is limited and slow
  • No centralized system for product categorization

docs.title: Solution Overview
docs.text:
You'll build a modern product catalog management system that includes:

  1. Centralized Product Database
  2. Structured product data with attributes
  3. Category hierarchy and tagging

  4. Product images and descriptions

  5. Real-Time Inventory Tracking

  6. Multi-warehouse inventory management
  7. Automatic stock level updates

  8. Low-stock alerts and notifications

  9. Customer-Facing Interface

  10. Product search and filtering
  11. Category browsing
  12. Real-time availability display

  13. Product detail pages

  14. Basic Order Processing

  15. Shopping cart functionality
  16. Inventory reservation on order
  17. Order status tracking

docs.title: Expected Outcomes
docs.text:
By completing this use case, you'll help RetailCo:

  • ✅ Reduce inventory discrepancies by 30%
  • ✅ Improve customer satisfaction with real-time availability
  • ✅ Increase operational efficiency by 40%
  • ✅ Enable faster product launches and updates

docs.title: Technical Components
docs.text:
You'll work with:

  • Product data models and schemas
  • Inventory management APIs
  • Search and filtering algorithms
  • Customer interface components
  • Order processing workflows

docs.title: Estimated Duration
docs.text:
• Setup: ~10 minutes
  • Implementation: ~30 minutes
  • Testing & Validation: ~10 minutes
  • Total: ~50 minutes

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• Completed Lab 101 Prerequisites
  • Understanding of basic retail concepts
  • Sample product dataset (provided)

docs.title: Ready to Start?
docs.text:
Proceed to the Lab Guide to begin building your e-commerce product catalog solution.

Learning Focus

This use case emphasizes data modeling, API integration, and real-time synchronization - core skills for any retail technology solution.

docs.title: Lab Guide: E-Commerce Product Catalog
docs.text:
Placeholder

Fill in product-specific implementation steps before running this track.

Follow this guide to build your e-commerce product catalog solution.

docs.title: Step 1: Set Up Product Data Model
docs.text: Create the foundational data structure for your product catalog.

docs.title: Tasks
docs.text:
1. Define product schema with required attributes
  2. Create category hierarchy structure
  3. Set up product-category relationships
  4. Configure product attributes (size, color, price, etc.)

docs.title: Implementation
docs.text:
# Example product schema
Product:
  id: string
  name: string
  description: text
  category_id: string
  price: decimal
  sku: string
  attributes: object
  images: array
  created_at: timestamp
Expected Output 
Product schema created successfully
- 5 product categories defined
- Product attributes configured
- Sample products imported

docs.title: Step 2: Configure Multi-Warehouse Inventory
docs.text: Set up inventory tracking across multiple warehouse locations.

docs.title: Tasks
docs.text:
1. Define warehouse locations
  2. Create inventory tracking system
  3. Set up stock level monitoring
  4. Configure low-stock alert thresholds

docs.title: Implementation
docs.text:
# Example inventory schema
Inventory:
  product_id: string
  warehouse_id: string
  quantity: integer
  reserved: integer
  available: integer
  last_updated: timestamp
Expected Output 
Inventory system configured
- 3 warehouse locations added
- Stock levels synchronized
- Alert thresholds set at 10 units

docs.title: Step 3: Build Product Search & Browse Interface
docs.text: Create customer-facing product discovery features.

docs.title: Tasks
docs.text:
1. Implement product search functionality
  2. Create category browsing interface

docs.title: Workflow
docs.text:
graph LR
    A[Add to Cart] --> B[Reserve Inventory]
    B --> C[Create Order]
    C --> D[Update Stock]
    D --> E[Send Confirmation]
Expected Output 
Order processing active
- Orders created successfully
- Inventory reserved automatically
- Stock levels update in real-time
- Confirmation emails sent

docs.title: Business Context
docs.text: RetailCo operates both physical stores and an online presence. They need a unified inventory management system that provides real-time visibility across all channels and automates reordering to prevent stockouts.

docs.title: Business Challenge
docs.text:
Currently, RetailCo faces several challenges:

  • Inventory data is siloed between online and physical stores
  • No real-time visibility of stock across all locations
  • Manual reordering leads to stockouts and overstock situations
  • Customers can't check in-store availability online
  • No automated alerts for low stock or reorder points

docs.title: Solution Overview
docs.text:
You'll build an omnichannel inventory management system that includes:

  1. Unified Inventory Platform
  2. Single source of truth for all inventory
  3. Real-time synchronization across channels

  4. Multi-location stock visibility

  5. Automated Reordering System

  6. Configurable reorder points
  7. Automatic purchase order generation

  8. Supplier integration

  9. Store Fulfillment Options

  10. Buy online, pick up in store (BOPIS)
  11. Ship from store capabilities

  12. Store-to-store transfers

  13. Analytics & Reporting

  14. Stock level dashboards
  15. Turnover rate analysis
  16. Demand forecasting

docs.title: Expected Outcomes
docs.text:
By completing this use case, you'll help RetailCo:

  • ✅ Achieve 99% inventory accuracy across all channels
  • ✅ Reduce stockouts by 45%
  • ✅ Decrease excess inventory by 30%
  • ✅ Enable seamless omnichannel customer experiences

docs.title: Technical Components
docs.text:
You'll work with:

  • Multi-location inventory data models
  • Real-time synchronization APIs
  • Automated reordering logic
  • Store fulfillment workflows
  • Analytics and reporting dashboards

docs.title: Estimated Duration
docs.text:
• Setup: ~10 minutes
  • Implementation: ~30 minutes
  • Testing & Validation: ~10 minutes
  • Total: ~50 minutes

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• Completed Lab 101 Prerequisites
  • Understanding of omnichannel retail operations
  • Sample inventory dataset (provided)

docs.title: Ready to Start?
docs.text:
Proceed to the Lab Guide to begin building your omnichannel inventory management solution.

Learning Focus

This use case emphasizes real-time data synchronization, automation, and multi-location management - essential skills for modern retail operations.

docs.title: Lab Guide: Omnichannel Inventory Management
docs.text:
Placeholder

Fill in product-specific implementation steps before running this track.

Follow this guide to build your omnichannel inventory management solution.

docs.title: Step 1: Set Up Multi-Location Inventory System
docs.text: Create a unified inventory platform that tracks stock across all locations.

docs.title: Tasks
docs.text:
1. Define location types (stores, warehouses, distribution centers)
  2. Create inventory tracking schema
  3. Set up real-time synchronization
  4. Configure location hierarchies

docs.title: Implementation
docs.text:
# Example location schema
Location:
  id: string
  name: string
  type: enum [store, warehouse, distribution_center]
  address: object
  capacity: integer
  active: boolean

# Example inventory schema
Inventory:
  product_id: string
  location_id: string
  quantity_on_hand: integer
  quantity_reserved: integer
  quantity_available: integer
  reorder_point: integer
  reorder_quantity: integer
  last_sync: timestamp
Expected Output 
Multi-location inventory configured
- 10 store locations added
- 2 warehouse locations added
- Real-time sync enabled
- Location hierarchies defined

docs.title: Step 2: Implement Automated Reordering Logic
docs.text: Set up intelligent reordering based on stock levels and demand patterns.

docs.title: Tasks
docs.text:
1. Configure reorder points for each product-location
  2. Create automated purchase order generation
  3. Set up supplier integration
  4. Implement approval workflows

docs.title: Reordering Logic
docs.text:
# Pseudocode for reorder logic
if quantity_available <= reorder_point:
    create_purchase_order(
        product_id=product_id,
        quantity=reorder_quantity,
        supplier_id=preferred_supplier,
        delivery_location=location_id
    )
    send_notification(purchasing_team)
Expected Output 
Automated reordering active
- Reorder points configured for 150 products
- Purchase orders auto-generated
- Supplier notifications sent
- Approval workflow functional

docs.title: Step 3: Build Store Fulfillment Capabilities
docs.text: Enable omnichannel fulfillment options for customers.

docs.title: Tasks
docs.text:
1. Implement BOPIS (Buy Online, Pick up In Store)
  2. Create ship-from-store functionality
  3. Set up store-to-store transfer workflows
  4. Build customer notification system

docs.title: Fulfillment Options
docs.text:
graph TD
    A[Customer Order] --> B{Fulfillment Option}
    B -->|BOPIS| C[Reserve at Store]
    B -->|Ship from Store| D[Ship from Nearest Store]
    B -->|Standard| E[Ship from Warehouse]
    C --> F[Customer Pickup]
    D --> G[Store Ships]
    E --> H[Warehouse Ships]
Expected Output 
Fulfillment options enabled
- BOPIS available for all stores
- Ship-from-store configured
- Store transfers automated
- Customer notifications active

docs.title: Step 4: Create Analytics Dashboard
docs.text: Build visibility into inventory performance and trends.

docs.title: Tasks
docs.text:
1. Create real-time inventory dashboard
  2. Implement stock level alerts
  3. Build turnover rate reports
  4. Set up demand forecasting

docs.title: Key Metrics
docs.text:
• Stock Levels: Current inventory by location
  • Turnover Rate: How quickly inventory sells
  • Stockout Rate: Frequency of out-of-stock situations
  • Excess Inventory: Slow-moving or obsolete stock
  • Forecast Accuracy: Predicted vs. actual demand
Expected Output 
Analytics dashboard deployed
- Real-time inventory visibility
- Automated alerts configured
- Turnover reports generated
- Demand forecasts calculated

docs.title: Test Scenarios
docs.text:
1. Multi-Location Sync
  2. Update inventory at one location
  3. Verify sync across all systems

  4. Check real-time accuracy

  5. Automated Reordering

  6. Reduce stock below reorder point
  7. Verify purchase order creation

  8. Check supplier notifications

  9. Omnichannel Fulfillment

  10. Place BOPIS order
  11. Test ship-from-store
  12. Verify store transfer workflow

docs.title: Success Criteria
docs.text:
• ✅ Inventory syncs in real-time across all locations
  • ✅ Automated reordering prevents stockouts
  • ✅ All fulfillment options working correctly
  • ✅ Analytics provide actionable insights

docs.title: Troubleshooting
docs.text:
Common issues and solutions:

Issue Solution Sync delays Check network connectivity and API rate limits Reorder not triggering Verify reorder points and logic configuration BOPIS unavailable Check store inventory and reservation system

For additional help, refer to the Troubleshooting Guide.

  • Share your experience with the facilitator

Well Done!

You've successfully built an omnichannel inventory management system with automated reordering and multi-location fulfillment capabilities.

  • Streamline sales processes and reduce paperwork

docs.title: Business Context
docs.text: RetailCo wants to modernize their product catalog system to support their growing e-commerce business. They need a centralized system to manage product information, track inventory across multiple warehouses, and provide customers with a seamless browsing experience.

docs.title: Business Challenge
docs.text:
Currently, RetailCo faces several challenges:

  • Product information is scattered across multiple spreadsheets
  • Inventory levels are updated manually, leading to overselling
  • Customers can't see real-time product availability
  • Search functionality is limited and slow
  • No centralized system for product categorization

docs.title: Solution Overview
docs.text:
You'll build a modern product catalog management system that includes:

  1. Centralized Product Database
  2. Structured product data with attributes
  3. Category hierarchy and tagging

  4. Product images and descriptions

  5. Real-Time Inventory Tracking

  6. Multi-warehouse inventory management
  7. Automatic stock level updates

  8. Low-stock alerts and notifications

  9. Customer-Facing Interface

  10. Product search and filtering
  11. Category browsing
  12. Real-time availability display

  13. Product detail pages

  14. Basic Order Processing

  15. Shopping cart functionality
  16. Inventory reservation on order
  17. Order status tracking

docs.title: Expected Outcomes
docs.text:
By completing this use case, you'll help RetailCo:

  • ✅ Reduce inventory discrepancies by 30%
  • ✅ Improve customer satisfaction with real-time availability
  • ✅ Increase operational efficiency by 40%
  • ✅ Enable faster product launches and updates

docs.title: Technical Components
docs.text:
You'll work with:

  • Product data models and schemas
  • Inventory management APIs
  • Search and filtering algorithms
  • Customer interface components
  • Order processing workflows

docs.title: Estimated Duration
docs.text:
• Setup: ~10 minutes
  • Implementation: ~30 minutes
  • Testing & Validation: ~10 minutes
  • Total: ~50 minutes

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• Completed Lab 101 Prerequisites
  • Understanding of basic retail concepts
  • Sample product dataset (provided)

docs.title: Ready to Start?
docs.text:
Proceed to the Lab Guide to begin building your e-commerce product catalog solution.

Learning Focus

This use case emphasizes data modeling, API integration, and real-time synchronization - core skills for any retail technology solution.

docs.title: Lab Guide: E-Commerce Product Catalog
docs.text:
Placeholder

Fill in product-specific implementation steps before running this track.

Follow this guide to build your e-commerce product catalog solution.

docs.title: Step 1: Set Up Product Data Model
docs.text: Create the foundational data structure for your product catalog.

docs.title: Tasks
docs.text:
1. Define product schema with required attributes
  2. Create category hierarchy structure
  3. Set up product-category relationships
  4. Configure product attributes (size, color, price, etc.)

docs.title: Implementation
docs.text:
# Example product schema
Product:
  id: string
  name: string
  description: text
  category_id: string
  price: decimal
  sku: string
  attributes: object
  images: array
  created_at: timestamp
Expected Output 
Product schema created successfully
- 5 product categories defined
- Product attributes configured
- Sample products imported

docs.title: Step 2: Configure Multi-Warehouse Inventory
docs.text: Set up inventory tracking across multiple warehouse locations.

docs.title: Tasks
docs.text:
1. Define warehouse locations
  2. Create inventory tracking system
  3. Set up stock level monitoring
  4. Configure low-stock alert thresholds

docs.title: Implementation
docs.text:
# Example inventory schema
Inventory:
  product_id: string
  warehouse_id: string
  quantity: integer
  reserved: integer
  available: integer
  last_updated: timestamp
Expected Output 
Inventory system configured
- 3 warehouse locations added
- Stock levels synchronized
- Alert thresholds set at 10 units

docs.title: Step 3: Build Product Search & Browse Interface
docs.text: Create customer-facing product discovery features.

docs.title: Tasks
docs.text:
1. Implement product search functionality
  2. Create category browsing interface
  3. Add filtering and sorting options
  4. Display real-time availability

docs.title: Key Features
docs.text:
• Full-text product search
  • Category-based navigation
  • Filter by price, attributes, availability
  • Sort by relevance, price, popularity
Expected Output 
Search interface deployed
- Search returns relevant results in <100ms
- Category navigation functional
- Filters working correctly
- Real-time availability displayed

docs.title: Step 4: Implement Order Processing Workflow
docs.text: Create basic order management with inventory reservation.

docs.title: Tasks
docs.text:
1. Build shopping cart functionality
  2. Implement inventory reservation on order
  3. Create order status tracking
  4. Set up order confirmation system

docs.title: Workflow
docs.text:
graph LR
    A[Add to Cart] --> B[Reserve Inventory]
    B --> C[Create Order]
    C --> D[Update Stock]
    D --> E[Send Confirmation]
Expected Output 
Order processing active
- Orders created successfully
- Inventory reserved automatically
- Stock levels update in real-time
- Confirmation emails sent

docs.title: Test Scenarios
docs.text:
1. Product Search
  2. Search for products by name
  3. Filter by category and price

  4. Verify search results accuracy

  5. Inventory Management

  6. Check stock levels across warehouses
  7. Verify low-stock alerts trigger

  8. Test inventory reservation on order

  9. Order Processing

  10. Create test orders
  11. Verify inventory updates
  12. Check order status tracking

docs.title: Success Criteria
docs.text:
• ✅ All products searchable and browsable
  • ✅ Inventory levels accurate across all warehouses
  • ✅ Orders process correctly with inventory updates
  • ✅ Real-time availability displayed to customers

docs.title: Troubleshooting
docs.text:
Common issues and solutions:

Issue Solution Search returns no results Check index configuration and data import Inventory not updating Verify API connections and webhook setup Orders failing Check inventory reservation logic

For additional help, refer to the Troubleshooting Guide.

docs.title: Business Context
docs.text: RetailCo operates both physical stores and an online presence. They need a unified inventory management system that provides real-time visibility across all channels and automates reordering to prevent stockouts.

docs.title: Business Challenge
docs.text:
Currently, RetailCo faces several challenges:

  • Inventory data is siloed between online and physical stores
  • No real-time visibility of stock across all locations
  • Manual reordering leads to stockouts and overstock situations
  • Customers can't check in-store availability online
  • No automated alerts for low stock or reorder points

docs.title: Solution Overview
docs.text:
You'll build an omnichannel inventory management system that includes:

  1. Unified Inventory Platform
  2. Single source of truth for all inventory
  3. Real-time synchronization across channels

  4. Multi-location stock visibility

  5. Automated Reordering System

  6. Configurable reorder points
  7. Automatic purchase order generation

  8. Supplier integration

  9. Store Fulfillment Options

  10. Buy online, pick up in store (BOPIS)
  11. Ship from store capabilities

  12. Store-to-store transfers

  13. Analytics & Reporting

  14. Stock level dashboards
  15. Turnover rate analysis
  16. Demand forecasting

docs.title: Expected Outcomes
docs.text:
By completing this use case, you'll help RetailCo:

  • ✅ Achieve 99% inventory accuracy across all channels
  • ✅ Reduce stockouts by 45%
  • ✅ Decrease excess inventory by 30%
  • ✅ Enable seamless omnichannel customer experiences

docs.title: Technical Components
docs.text:
You'll work with:

  • Multi-location inventory data models
  • Real-time synchronization APIs
  • Automated reordering logic
  • Store fulfillment workflows
  • Analytics and reporting dashboards

docs.title: Estimated Duration
docs.text:
• Setup: ~10 minutes
  • Implementation: ~30 minutes
  • Testing & Validation: ~10 minutes
  • Total: ~50 minutes

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• Completed Lab 101 Prerequisites
  • Understanding of omnichannel retail operations
  • Sample inventory dataset (provided)

docs.title: Ready to Start?
docs.text:
Proceed to the Lab Guide to begin building your omnichannel inventory management solution.

Learning Focus

This use case emphasizes real-time data synchronization, automation, and multi-location management - essential skills for modern retail operations.

docs.title: Lab Guide: Omnichannel Inventory Management
docs.text:
Placeholder

Fill in product-specific implementation steps before running this track.

Follow this guide to build your omnichannel inventory management solution.

docs.title: Step 1: Set Up Multi-Location Inventory System
docs.text: Create a unified inventory platform that tracks stock across all locations.

docs.title: Tasks
docs.text:
1. Define location types (stores, warehouses, distribution centers)
  2. Create inventory tracking schema
  3. Set up real-time synchronization
  4. Configure location hierarchies

docs.title: Implementation
docs.text:
# Example location schema
Location:
  id: string
  name: string
  type: enum [store, warehouse, distribution_center]
  address: object
  capacity: integer
  active: boolean

# Example inventory schema
Inventory:
  product_id: string
  location_id: string
  quantity_on_hand: integer
  quantity_reserved: integer
  quantity_available: integer
  reorder_point: integer
  reorder_quantity: integer
  last_sync: timestamp
Expected Output 
Multi-location inventory configured
- 10 store locations added
- 2 warehouse locations added
- Real-time sync enabled
- Location hierarchies defined

docs.title: Step 2: Implement Automated Reordering Logic
docs.text: Set up intelligent reordering based on stock levels and demand patterns.

docs.title: Tasks
docs.text:
1. Configure reorder points for each product-location
  2. Create automated purchase order generation
  3. Set up supplier integration
  4. Implement approval workflows

docs.title: Reordering Logic
docs.text:
# Pseudocode for reorder logic
if quantity_available <= reorder_point:
    create_purchase_order(
        product_id=product_id,
        quantity=reorder_quantity,
        supplier_id=preferred_supplier,
        delivery_location=location_id
    )
    send_notification(purchasing_team)
Expected Output 
Automated reordering active
- Reorder points configured for 150 products
- Purchase orders auto-generated
- Supplier notifications sent
- Approval workflow functional

docs.title: Step 3: Build Store Fulfillment Capabilities
docs.text: Enable omnichannel fulfillment options for customers.

docs.title: Tasks
docs.text:
1. Implement BOPIS (Buy Online, Pick up In Store)
  2. Create ship-from-store functionality
  3. Set up store-to-store transfer workflows
  4. Build customer notification system

docs.title: Fulfillment Options
docs.text:
graph TD
    A[Customer Order] --> B{Fulfillment Option}
    B -->|BOPIS| C[Reserve at Store]
    B -->|Ship from Store| D[Ship from Nearest Store]
    B -->|Standard| E[Ship from Warehouse]
    C --> F[Customer Pickup]
    D --> G[Store Ships]
    E --> H[Warehouse Ships]
Expected Output 
Fulfillment options enabled
- BOPIS available for all stores
- Ship-from-store configured
- Store transfers automated
- Customer notifications active

docs.title: Step 4: Create Analytics Dashboard
docs.text: Build visibility into inventory performance and trends.

docs.title: Tasks
docs.text:
1. Create real-time inventory dashboard
  2. Implement stock level alerts
  3. Build turnover rate reports
  4. Set up demand forecasting

docs.title: Key Metrics
docs.text:
• Stock Levels: Current inventory by location
  • Turnover Rate: How quickly inventory sells
  • Stockout Rate: Frequency of out-of-stock situations
  • Excess Inventory: Slow-moving or obsolete stock
  • Forecast Accuracy: Predicted vs. actual demand
Expected Output 
Analytics dashboard deployed
- Real-time inventory visibility
- Automated alerts configured
- Turnover reports generated
- Demand forecasts calculated

docs.title: Test Scenarios
docs.text:
1. Multi-Location Sync
  2. Update inventory at one location
  3. Verify sync across all systems

  4. Check real-time accuracy

  5. Automated Reordering

  6. Reduce stock below reorder point
  7. Verify purchase order creation

  8. Check supplier notifications

  9. Omnichannel Fulfillment

  10. Place BOPIS order
  11. Test ship-from-store
  12. Verify store transfer workflow

docs.title: Success Criteria
docs.text:
• ✅ Inventory syncs in real-time across all locations
  • ✅ Automated reordering prevents stockouts
  • ✅ All fulfillment options working correctly
  • ✅ Analytics provide actionable insights

docs.title: Troubleshooting
docs.text:
Common issues and solutions:

Issue Solution Sync delays Check network connectivity and API rate limits Reorder not triggering Verify reorder points and logic configuration BOPIS unavailable Check store inventory and reservation system

For additional help, refer to the Troubleshooting Guide.

  • Continue to Lab 201 for advanced features
  • Share your experience with the facilitator

Well Done!

You've successfully built an omnichannel inventory management system with automated reordering and multi-location fulfillment capabilities.

Domain Context

docs.title: Scenario

docs.title: What You'll Build

  • [ ] Inventory is reserved upon order creation
  • [ ] Order status updates correctly
  • [ ] End-to-end order flow completes

docs.title: Lab 101 — Retail Domain
docs.text:
Domain Context

In this lab, you'll build foundational retail solutions. Choose a use case that aligns with your business interests. Each use case provides hands-on experience with the same technical concepts applied to different retail scenarios.

docs.title: Scenario
docs.text: You're working with RetailCo, a mid-sized retail company looking to modernize their digital presence. They need solutions across multiple areas of their business operations.

docs.title: Available Use Cases
docs.text:
Select a use case to begin your hands-on lab:

  • E-Commerce Product Catalog

    Build a product catalog management system with inventory tracking and customer-facing browsing interface.

    What You'll Build: Product data models, inventory APIs, search functionality

    View Use Case Details

  • Omnichannel Inventory Management

    Create a multi-location inventory system with real-time stock tracking and automated reordering.

    What You'll Build: Multi-location inventory, stock alerts, order management

    View Use Case Details

docs.title: Learning Objectives
docs.text:
Regardless of which use case you choose, you will learn:

  • Core product concepts and architecture
  • Data modeling and schema design
  • API integration patterns
  • Basic user interface development
  • Real-time data synchronization

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text: Before starting any use case, ensure you have completed the general Lab 101 Prerequisites.

docs.title: Retail-Specific Requirements
docs.text:
• [ ] Understanding of basic retail concepts (SKU, inventory, orders)
  • [ ] Familiarity with e-commerce workflows
  • [ ] Sample retail dataset (provided by facilitator)

Use Case Selection

Choose the use case that best matches your business interests. Both use cases cover the same technical learning objectives but with different business contexts.

docs.title: What You Built
docs.text:
In this lab, you successfully built a foundational retail solution for RetailCo, including:

  • Product Catalog Management: Centralized product information with categories and attributes
  • Inventory Tracking: Multi-location inventory management with real-time updates
  • Customer Interface: Product browsing and search functionality
  • Order Processing: Basic order workflow with inventory reservation

  • Organizing products with categories and attributes
  • Managing product information across channels

docs.title: Inventory Operations
docs.text:

docs.title: Customer Experience
docs.text:
• Building intuitive product browsing interfaces

docs.title: Order Workflows

docs.text:
Your solution helps RetailCo:

  • ✅ Reduce inventory discrepancies through real-time tracking
  • ✅ Improve customer satisfaction with accurate availability

docs.title: Business Context
docs.text: RetailCo wants to modernize their product catalog system to support their growing e-commerce business. They need a centralized system to manage product information, track inventory across multiple warehouses, and provide customers with a seamless browsing experience.

docs.title: Business Challenge
docs.text:
Currently, RetailCo faces several challenges:

  • Product information is scattered across multiple spreadsheets
  • Inventory levels are updated manually, leading to overselling
  • Customers can't see real-time product availability
  • Search functionality is limited and slow
  • No centralized system for product categorization

docs.title: Solution Overview
docs.text:
You'll build a modern product catalog management system that includes:

  1. Centralized Product Database
  2. Structured product data with attributes
  3. Category hierarchy and tagging

  4. Product images and descriptions

  5. Real-Time Inventory Tracking

  6. Multi-warehouse inventory management
  7. Automatic stock level updates

  8. Low-stock alerts and notifications

  9. Customer-Facing Interface

  10. Product search and filtering
  11. Category browsing
  12. Real-time availability display

  13. Product detail pages

  14. Basic Order Processing

  15. Shopping cart functionality
  16. Inventory reservation on order
  17. Order status tracking

docs.title: Expected Outcomes
docs.text:
By completing this use case, you'll help RetailCo:

  • ✅ Reduce inventory discrepancies by 30%
  • ✅ Improve customer satisfaction with real-time availability
  • ✅ Increase operational efficiency by 40%
  • ✅ Enable faster product launches and updates

docs.title: Technical Components
docs.text:
You'll work with:

  • Product data models and schemas
  • Inventory management APIs
  • Search and filtering algorithms
  • Customer interface components
  • Order processing workflows

docs.title: Estimated Duration
docs.text:
• Setup: ~10 minutes
  • Implementation: ~30 minutes
  • Testing & Validation: ~10 minutes
  • Total: ~50 minutes

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• Completed Lab 101 Prerequisites
  • Understanding of basic retail concepts
  • Sample product dataset (provided)

docs.title: Ready to Start?
docs.text:
Proceed to the Lab Guide to begin building your e-commerce product catalog solution.

Learning Focus

This use case emphasizes data modeling, API integration, and real-time synchronization - core skills for any retail technology solution.

docs.title: Lab Guide: E-Commerce Product Catalog
docs.text:
Placeholder

Fill in product-specific implementation steps before running this track.

Follow this guide to build your e-commerce product catalog solution.

docs.title: Step 1: Set Up Product Data Model
docs.text: Create the foundational data structure for your product catalog.

docs.title: Tasks
docs.text:
1. Define product schema with required attributes
  2. Create category hierarchy structure
  3. Set up product-category relationships
  4. Configure product attributes (size, color, price, etc.)

docs.title: Implementation
docs.text:
# Example product schema
Product:
  id: string
  name: string
  description: text
  category_id: string
  price: decimal
  sku: string
  attributes: object
  images: array
  created_at: timestamp
Expected Output 
Product schema created successfully
- 5 product categories defined
- Product attributes configured
- Sample products imported

docs.title: Step 2: Configure Multi-Warehouse Inventory
docs.text: Set up inventory tracking across multiple warehouse locations.

docs.title: Tasks
docs.text:
1. Define warehouse locations
  2. Create inventory tracking system
  3. Set up stock level monitoring
  4. Configure low-stock alert thresholds

docs.title: Implementation
docs.text:
# Example inventory schema
Inventory:
  product_id: string
  warehouse_id: string
  quantity: integer
  reserved: integer
  available: integer
  last_updated: timestamp
Expected Output 
Inventory system configured
- 3 warehouse locations added
- Stock levels synchronized
- Alert thresholds set at 10 units

docs.title: Step 3: Build Product Search & Browse Interface
docs.text: Create customer-facing product discovery features.

docs.title: Tasks

  2. Create category browsing interface

  4. Display real-time availability

docs.text:
• Full-text product search
  • Category-based navigation
  • Filter by price, attributes, availability
  • Sort by relevance, price, popularity
Expected Output 
Search interface deployed
- Search returns relevant results in <100ms
- Category navigation functional
- Filters working correctly
- Real-time availability displayed

docs.text: Create basic order management with inventory reservation.

  2. Implement inventory reservation on order
  3. Create order status tracking
  4. Set up order confirmation system

docs.title: Workflow
docs.text:
graph LR
    A[Add to Cart] --> B[Reserve Inventory]
    B --> C[Create Order]
    C --> D[Update Stock]
    D --> E[Send Confirmation]
Expected Output 
Order processing active
- Orders created successfully
- Inventory reserved automatically
- Stock levels update in real-time
- Confirmation emails sent

docs.title: Business Context
docs.text: RetailCo operates both physical stores and an online presence. They need a unified inventory management system that provides real-time visibility across all channels and automates reordering to prevent stockouts.

docs.title: Business Challenge
docs.text:
Currently, RetailCo faces several challenges:

  • Inventory data is siloed between online and physical stores
  • No real-time visibility of stock across all locations
  • Manual reordering leads to stockouts and overstock situations
  • Customers can't check in-store availability online
  • No automated alerts for low stock or reorder points

docs.title: Solution Overview
docs.text:
You'll build an omnichannel inventory management system that includes:

  1. Unified Inventory Platform
  2. Single source of truth for all inventory
  3. Real-time synchronization across channels

  4. Multi-location stock visibility

  5. Automated Reordering System

  6. Configurable reorder points
  7. Automatic purchase order generation

  8. Supplier integration

  9. Store Fulfillment Options

  10. Buy online, pick up in store (BOPIS)
  11. Ship from store capabilities

  12. Store-to-store transfers

  13. Analytics & Reporting

  14. Stock level dashboards
  15. Turnover rate analysis
  16. Demand forecasting

docs.title: Expected Outcomes
docs.text:
By completing this use case, you'll help RetailCo:

  • ✅ Achieve 99% inventory accuracy across all channels
  • ✅ Reduce stockouts by 45%
  • ✅ Decrease excess inventory by 30%
  • ✅ Enable seamless omnichannel customer experiences

docs.title: Technical Components
docs.text:
You'll work with:

  • Multi-location inventory data models
  • Real-time synchronization APIs
  • Automated reordering logic
  • Store fulfillment workflows
  • Analytics and reporting dashboards

docs.title: Estimated Duration
docs.text:
• Setup: ~10 minutes
  • Implementation: ~30 minutes
  • Testing & Validation: ~10 minutes
  • Total: ~50 minutes

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• Completed Lab 101 Prerequisites
  • Understanding of omnichannel retail operations
  • Sample inventory dataset (provided)

docs.text:
Proceed to the Lab Guide to begin building your omnichannel inventory management solution.

Learning Focus

This use case emphasizes real-time data synchronization, automation, and multi-location management - essential skills for modern retail operations.

docs.title: Lab Guide: Omnichannel Inventory Management
docs.text:
Placeholder

Fill in product-specific implementation steps before running this track.

Follow this guide to build your omnichannel inventory management solution.

docs.title: Step 1: Set Up Multi-Location Inventory System
docs.text: Create a unified inventory platform that tracks stock across all locations.

docs.title: Tasks
docs.text:
1. Define location types (stores, warehouses, distribution centers)
  2. Create inventory tracking schema
  3. Set up real-time synchronization
  4. Configure location hierarchies

docs.title: Implementation
docs.text:
# Example location schema
Location:
  id: string
  name: string
  type: enum [store, warehouse, distribution_center]
  address: object
  capacity: integer
  active: boolean

# Example inventory schema
Inventory:
  product_id: string
  location_id: string
  quantity_on_hand: integer
  quantity_reserved: integer
  quantity_available: integer
  reorder_point: integer
  reorder_quantity: integer
  last_sync: timestamp
Expected Output 
Multi-location inventory configured
- 10 store locations added
- 2 warehouse locations added
- Real-time sync enabled
- Location hierarchies defined

docs.title: Step 2: Implement Automated Reordering Logic
docs.text: Set up intelligent reordering based on stock levels and demand patterns.

docs.title: Tasks
docs.text:
1. Configure reorder points for each product-location
  2. Create automated purchase order generation
  3. Set up supplier integration
  4. Implement approval workflows

docs.title: Reordering Logic
docs.text:
# Pseudocode for reorder logic
if quantity_available <= reorder_point:
    create_purchase_order(
        product_id=product_id,
        quantity=reorder_quantity,
        supplier_id=preferred_supplier,
        delivery_location=location_id
    )
    send_notification(purchasing_team)
Expected Output 
Automated reordering active
- Reorder points configured for 150 products
- Purchase orders auto-generated
- Supplier notifications sent
- Approval workflow functional

docs.title: Step 3: Build Store Fulfillment Capabilities
docs.text: Enable omnichannel fulfillment options for customers.

docs.title: Tasks
docs.text:
1. Implement BOPIS (Buy Online, Pick up In Store)
  2. Create ship-from-store functionality
  3. Set up store-to-store transfer workflows
  4. Build customer notification system

docs.title: Fulfillment Options
docs.text:
graph TD
    A[Customer Order] --> B{Fulfillment Option}
    B -->|BOPIS| C[Reserve at Store]
    B -->|Ship from Store| D[Ship from Nearest Store]
    B -->|Standard| E[Ship from Warehouse]
    C --> F[Customer Pickup]
    D --> G[Store Ships]
    E --> H[Warehouse Ships]
Expected Output 
Fulfillment options enabled
- BOPIS available for all stores
- Ship-from-store configured
- Store transfers automated
- Customer notifications active

docs.title: Step 4: Create Analytics Dashboard
docs.text: Build visibility into inventory performance and trends.

docs.title: Tasks
docs.text:
1. Create real-time inventory dashboard
  2. Implement stock level alerts
  3. Build turnover rate reports
  4. Set up demand forecasting

docs.title: Key Metrics
docs.text:
• Stock Levels: Current inventory by location
  • Turnover Rate: How quickly inventory sells
  • Stockout Rate: Frequency of out-of-stock situations
  • Excess Inventory: Slow-moving or obsolete stock
  • Forecast Accuracy: Predicted vs. actual demand
Expected Output 
Analytics dashboard deployed
- Real-time inventory visibility
- Automated alerts configured
- Turnover reports generated
- Demand forecasts calculated

docs.title: Test Scenarios
docs.text:
1. Multi-Location Sync
  2. Update inventory at one location
  3. Verify sync across all systems

  4. Check real-time accuracy

  5. Automated Reordering

  6. Reduce stock below reorder point
  7. Verify purchase order creation

  8. Check supplier notifications

  9. Omnichannel Fulfillment

  10. Place BOPIS order
  11. Test ship-from-store
  12. Verify store transfer workflow

docs.title: Success Criteria
docs.text:
• ✅ Inventory syncs in real-time across all locations
  • ✅ Automated reordering prevents stockouts
  • ✅ All fulfillment options working correctly
  • ✅ Analytics provide actionable insights

docs.title: Troubleshooting
docs.text:
Common issues and solutions:

Issue Solution Sync delays Check network connectivity and API rate limits Reorder not triggering Verify reorder points and logic configuration BOPIS unavailable Check store inventory and reservation system

For additional help, refer to the Troubleshooting Guide.

docs.title: Product Overview
docs.text:
Placeholder

Track 1 - Boost productivity with AI Agents consists of watsonx Orchestrate, watsonx.governance and IBM Bob

docs.title: What is watsonx Orchestrate?
docs.text:
IBM watsonx Orchestrate is a platform that helps organizations automate work using AI agents.

In simple terms, it lets you create digital assistants that can understand requests, connect to your business tools, and carry out tasks end-to-end like pulling data, updating systems, or triggering workflows without manual effort.

It works by orchestrating multi-agents and tools behind the scenes. These agents can collaborate, decide what steps to take, and execute tasks across different applications, while the platform manages the flow and routing of work.

You can build these agents using low-code interfaces or pro-code options, reuse prebuilt “skills” from a catalog, and integrate them with existing enterprise systems without replacing them.

Overall, it acts as an orchestration layer for enterprise AI—connecting data, apps, and workflows so routine work gets automated and teams can focus on higher-value tasks.

View the presentation slides

docs.title: What is watsonx.governance?
docs.text:
IBM watsonx.governance is a platform that helps organizations control, monitor, and manage how their AI systems are built and used so they remain safe, compliant, and trustworthy.

In simple terms, it acts as a “governance layer” for AI. It keeps track of how models are created, what data they use, and how they behave in production, while ensuring they follow company policies and external regulations.

It provides end-to-end oversight across the AI lifecycle from development to deployment so teams can monitor performance, detect issues like bias or drift, and maintain transparency in decision making.

The platform also helps manage risk and compliance by automating workflows, maintaining audit trails, and aligning AI systems with regulatory standards. This reduces manual effort and makes it easier to meet evolving legal and governance requirements.

Overall, it enables organizations to scale AI confidently by ensuring their models are reliable, explainable, and governed throughout their lifecycle.

View the presentation slides

docs.title: What is IBM Bob?
docs.text:
IBM Bob is an AI-powered development assistant that helps teams build, understand, and maintain software across the entire development lifecycle.

In simple terms, it acts like a smart coding partner. You can describe what you want in natural language, and it can generate code, explain existing code, fix issues, or create documentation without needing to manually dig through complex systems.

It goes beyond basic code generation by working across the full software lifecycle from planning and design to testing, deployment, and modernization of legacy systems. It can analyze large codebases, suggest improvements, and even help translate or refactor older technologies into modern ones.

IBM Bob is also designed to fit into existing developer workflows like IDEs and command-line tools and can automate repetitive tasks such as debugging, documentation, and code reviews.

Overall, it acts as an AI development partner helping teams move faster, reduce manual effort, and handle complex codebases more confidently.

View the presentation slides

docs.title: Troubleshooting
docs.text:
Common issues and solutions:

Issue Solution Terraform workspace not accessible Verify authentication tokens and organization access Monitoring data not appearing Check agent installation and network connectivity Vault sealed/unavailable Unseal Vault and verify cluster health NS1 DNS propagation delays Allow time for DNS propagation (up to 5 minutes) Maximo connection timeout Verify network access and instance status WebMethods integration failing Check endpoint configuration and credentials

For additional support, see the Track 03 Troubleshooting Guide.

docs.title: Lab Environment Setup — Track 04
docs.text: Provision and validate all lab environments at least 24 hours before the session. Never leave this to the morning of.

docs.title: Track 04 Products
docs.text:
This track focuses on:

  • PowerVS
  • Fusion
  • Flush

Work in progress

This track is currently under development. Environment setup details will be finalized as the track content is completed.

docs.title: Environment Requirements
docs.text: Requirement Detail Platform IBM Cloud / Hybrid Cloud / On-premises Accounts needed One per attendee (~20 total) Pre-provisioned resources PowerVS instancesFusion accessFlush configurationHybrid cloud connectivity Network Stable internet, no restrictive firewallVPN access (if required)Direct Link or Transit Gateway (for hybrid scenarios) Attendee machine Any OS with a modern browser (Chrome/Firefox/Edge)Terminal access for CLI operationsSSH clientVPN client (if required)

docs.title: Step 1 — Provision PowerVS Instances
docs.text:
# Provision PowerVS instances for each attendee
# Replace with actual IBM Cloud CLI commands or provisioning script
ibmcloud login --apikey <API_KEY>
ibmcloud target -r <REGION>

# Create PowerVS instances
for i in {1..20}; do
  echo "Provisioning PowerVS instance for user$i"
  # Add actual provisioning command here
done

docs.title: Step 2 — Configure Fusion Access
docs.text:
Set up Fusion platform access:

# Configure Fusion access for each user
echo "Setting up Fusion access for all users"

docs.title: Step 3 — Set Up Flush Configuration
docs.text:
Configure Flush for hybrid cloud scenarios:

# Set up Flush configuration
echo "Configuring Flush for hybrid cloud connectivity"

docs.title: Step 4 — Configure Hybrid Cloud Connectivity
docs.text:
Set up network connectivity for hybrid scenarios:

# Configure Direct Link or Transit Gateway
echo "Setting up hybrid cloud connectivity"

# Configure VPN access if needed
echo "Setting up VPN access"

docs.title: Step 5 — Validate Access
docs.text:
Run this check for each account before the session:

# Validation script for Track 04 environments
#!/bin/bash

for i in {1..20}; do
  echo "Validating environment for user$i"

  # Check PowerVS instance access
  # Check Fusion access
  # Check Flush configuration
  # Verify hybrid cloud connectivity
  # Test network latency

  echo "✓ user$i environment validated"
done

docs.title: Step 6 — Prepare Credentials Pack
docs.text:
Create a simple table with one row per attendee:

Seat Username Password PowerVS URL Fusion URL VPN Config SSH Key 1 user01 xxxxxxxx https://... https://... vpn-config-01 ssh-key-01 2 user02 xxxxxxxx https://... https://... vpn-config-02 ssh-key-02 ... ... ... ... ... ... ...

Warning

Distribute credentials on the day — not in advance — to avoid pre-session tinkering that breaks the environment state.

docs.title: Pre-Lab Checklist
docs.text:
Before each lab session, verify:

  • [ ] All PowerVS instances are running
  • [ ] Fusion access is functional
  • [ ] Flush configuration is correct
  • [ ] Hybrid cloud connectivity is working
  • [ ] VPN access is configured (if required)
  • [ ] SSH keys are distributed
  • [ ] Network latency is acceptable
  • [ ] Credentials are prepared and secured

docs.title: Lab 201 — Fundamentals
docs.text:
• Basic PowerVS configurations
  • Fusion platform access

docs.title: Lab 301 — Intermediate
docs.text:
• Advanced hybrid cloud scenarios
  • Multi-cloud connectivity

docs.title: Lab 401 — Advanced
docs.text:
• Production-like hybrid environment
  • Full disaster recovery setup

docs.title: Teardown
docs.text:
After the session, deprovision all environments within 48 hours to avoid unnecessary cost or license usage.

#!/bin/bash
# Teardown script for Track 04 environments

for i in {1..20}; do
  echo "Deprovisioning environment for user$i"

  # Delete PowerVS instances
  # Revoke Fusion access
  # Clean up Flush configurations
  # Remove VPN access
  # Revoke SSH keys

  echo "✓ user$i environment deprovisioned"
done

docs.title: Troubleshooting
docs.text:
Common issues and solutions:

Issue Solution Cannot access PowerVS Verify IBM Cloud authentication and region Fusion connection failing Check network access and authentication Hybrid connectivity issues Verify Direct Link/Transit Gateway status VPN connection timeout Check VPN configuration and firewall rules High network latency Verify network path and consider region proximity

For additional support, see the Track 04 Troubleshooting Guide.

docs.title: Troubleshooting — Track 02
docs.text: This page contains common issues and solutions for Track 02 labs.

docs.title: Issue: Lab environment not accessible
docs.text:
Symptoms: - Cannot access lab environment - Connection timeout errors

Solution: 1. Verify your network connection 2. Check if the lab environment is provisioned 3. Contact the facilitator if issues persist

This lab introduces intermediate concepts and more complex scenarios with [Product Name].

Duration: ~90 minutes

docs.title: Learning Objectives
docs.text:
By the end of this lab, you will be able to:

  • [ ] Objective 1
  • [ ] Objective 2
  • [ ] Objective 3
  • [ ] Objective 4

docs.title: Lab Structure
docs.text:
1. Setup — Prepare your environment
  2. Exercise 1 — [Exercise name]
  3. Exercise 2 — [Exercise name]
  4. Exercise 3 — [Exercise name]
  5. Validation — Verify your work

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
Before starting this lab, ensure you have:

  • Completed Lab 201
  • Access to the lab environment
  • Required tools installed (see Prerequisites)

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
1. Review Prerequisites
  2. Follow the Step-by-step Instructions
  3. Complete Validation
  4. Review the Recap

Tip
